summary: Matched a substantial slice of grCorneria by replacing multiple placeholder functions with C implementations, tightening Corneria/Venom shared stage behavior, and splitting ambiguous ground variable usage into arwing, smashtaunt, and venom-specific structs. The PR also renamed previously generic symbols such as fn_801DD65C and fn_801E24AC to stage-meaningful names, updated headers and symbol config, and adjusted grVenom to call the newly named shared smash taunt helper.
what_changed: Implemented multiple previously undecompiled grCorneria functions, including grCorneria_801DD2C0, grCorneria_801DD478, grCorneria_Arwing_801DE4C0, smashTaunt_801E24AC, smashTaunt_801E2550, grCorneria_801E2A6C, grCorneria_801E2AF4, grCorneria_801E2B80, grCorneria_801E2C34, grCorneria_801E2D14, grCorneria_801E2E50, and grCorneria_801E2FCC.
Renamed fn_801DD65C to grCorneria_801DD65C and updated the Ground_801C10B8 callback registration to use the new name.
Renamed grCorneria_801DE4C0 to grCorneria_Arwing_801DE4C0, reflecting that the function initializes Arwing ground variables and joint/list state.
Renamed fn_801E24AC and grCorneria_801E2550 to smashTaunt_801E24AC and smashTaunt_801E2550 because the render/init behavior is shared by Corneria and Venom smash taunt logic.
Added grArwing_GroundVars, grSmashTaunt_GroundVars, and grVenom_GroundVars to src/melee/gr/types.h and added them to the GroundVars union.
Changed Corneria code from using gv.corneria fields for arwing and smash taunt state to gv.arwing and gv.smashtaunt where appropriate.
Updated grVenom_80206B94 to call smashTaunt_801E2550 with gp->gv.smashtaunt instead of gp->gv.corneria.
Updated grVenom prototypes for grVenom_80206BF0 and grVenom_80206CB0 from unknown signatures to bool grVenom_80206BF0(int) and void grVenom_80206CB0(int), matching Corneria cross-stage calls.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to reflect the new grCorneria_801DD65C, grCorneria_Arwing_801DE4C0, smashTaunt_801E24AC, and smashTaunt_801E2550 symbol names.
Added required includes for camera, game mode helpers, ground display, grVenom, mplib, and baselib random to support the newly matched functions.
smart_moves: Separated overlapping ground variable interpretations into dedicated structs instead of continuing to overload grCorneria_GroundVars. This made the decompiled code more semantically accurate and supported both matching and future readability.
Identified that smashTaunt_801E24AC is shared by Corneria and Venom, then renamed and wired it as a shared helper rather than keeping it Corneria-specific.

Used stage_info.internal_stage_id branches in grCorneria_801E2A6C, grCorneria_801E2AF4, grCorneria_801E2B80, grCorneria_801E2C34, and grCorneria_801E2D14 to model the same public helper handling both CORNERIA and VENOM behavior.

Preserved matching-sensitive details visible in the diff, including PAD_STACK annotations, HSD_ASSERT line number usage, and a localized #pragma dont_inline block around grCorneria_801E2B80 and grCorneria_801E2C34.
Renamed symbols in the config file together with C/header declarations, keeping generated symbol names, prototypes, and implementation names aligned.
decomp_lessons: When a stage file contains behavior reused by a sibling stage, do not assume the ground variable union member should remain named after the current file. Introduce a semantic union member such as smashtaunt or arwing when the field layout represents a shared or entity-specific subsystem.
For stage object helpers that branch on stage_info.internal_stage_id, matching may require modeling cross-stage behavior directly rather than splitting the logic prematurely into per-stage wrappers.

Callback functions such as render callbacks and Ground_801C10B8 cleanup callbacks benefit from meaningful names once their role is understood, because their call sites often clarify the underlying subsystem.

Small header signature refinements matter for matching and reuse: this PR changed unknown prototypes to concrete bool, void, int, f32, Vec3*, HSD_JObj*, and Ground_GObj* signatures where evidence from implementation was available.

Stage matching work may require simultaneous changes to source, header, type union layout, and symbols.txt. Leaving any one of those stale can preserve misleading names or block consumers such as grVenom.
PAD_STACK and pragma controls are valid matching tactics when the decompiled control flow is understood but compiler layout/inlining still needs to be coerced.
naming_conventions: Use grCorneria_801xxxxx for address-based Corneria functions when no stronger semantic name is available.
Use subsystem-qualified names when the function role is clear, as in grCorneria_Arwing_801DE4C0 for Arwing-specific Corneria behavior.
Use shared subsystem names without a stage prefix when behavior is shared across stages, as in smashTaunt_801E24AC and smashTaunt_801E2550.
Ground variable structs follow the pattern struct gr<Name>_GroundVars, shown by grArwing_GroundVars, grSmashTaunt_GroundVars, and grVenom_GroundVars.
Ground union members use concise lowercase semantic names matching the subsystem or stage: arwing, smashtaunt, venom, corneria.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK annotations with specific byte sizes in newly matched functions, including 8, 20, 32, and 44 byte stack pads.
Used #pragma push, #pragma dont_inline on, and #pragma pop around grCorneria_801E2B80 and grCorneria_801E2C34, implying inlining control was necessary for matching those functions.
Kept an HSD_ASSERT with line number 3598 in the Arwing spawn helper paths, preserving matching-sensitive assert metadata.
Used M2C_FIELD on gp->gv.smashtaunt.xE4 at offset 0x4D in smashTaunt_801E24AC, indicating the pointed-to object type was still not fully typed but a known byte field was needed for a match.
Clamped Arwing selection values in grCorneria_801E0F34 to the range 0 through 0x13 before storing into gp->gv.arwing.xC4.
Used explicit -3.4028235e38f as the fallback return in grCorneria_801E2FCC, likely matching the original sentinel float.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The context reports one issue comment, but no human text excerpt was included, so no concrete reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that fully type the object stored in grSmashTaunt_GroundVars.xE4 and remove the M2C_FIELD access at offset 0x4D.
Search for additional users of grArwing_GroundVars to confirm whether grVenom_GroundVars and grArwing_GroundVars can share a common layout or need distinct semantic structs.

Inspect later grCorneria PRs for matches of the remaining placeholder functions around grCorneria_801DCCFC, grCorneria_801DCE1C, grCorneria_801DD350, grCorneria_801DDE88, grCorneria_801E277C, and grCorneria_801E2D90.
Check whether later review or cleanup PRs rename smashTaunt to a project-preferred capitalization or module prefix.
Search for other stage files with shared smash taunt render callbacks to reuse the grSmashTaunt_GroundVars pattern.
